name: Mustard Pickle
recipeIngredient: 2 quarts green tomatoes, cut into 1-inch chunks
1 bunch celery stalks, cut into 1/2-inch pieces
4 sweet red peppers, seeded, cut into 1-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Place all vegetables in a large enamel kettle and sprinkle with pickling salt.
2. Pour ice water over all the ingredients.
3. Let stand overnight.
4. Bring to a boil, then drain thoroughly.
5. Divide evenly between two preserving kettles.
6. Mix sugar, flour, mustard, and turmeric together.
7. Stir boiling hot vinegar into the flour mixture and mix well.
8. Divide evenly between two kettles of vegetables.
9. Working with one kettle of vegetables at a time, bring to a boil.
10. Boil gently for 5 minutes, stirring well to prevent flour from lumping.
11. Ladle into sterile pint jars, leaving 1/2-inch headroom.
12. Adjust lids and process in a boiling water bath for 15 minutes.
13. Remove and complete seals. Makes 12 pints, about 192 servings of two tablespoons each.
